channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame’s resurrection: Its chief architect on rebuilding France’s 'heart' in 5 years
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The day after the inferno struck Notre Dame on April 15, 2019, Philippe Villeneuve walked despondently into the remains of his cathedral. Smoke choked the spring air, the spire lay in rubble, and charred beams littered the nave.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Yoon replaces the defense minister as South Korea's parliament moves to vote on their impeachments
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s president replaced his defense minister Thursday as opposition parties moved to impeach both men over the stunning-but-brief imposition of martial law that brought armed troops into Seoul streets.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Daniel Penny trial: 3 pieces of evidence jurors asked to see again during deliberations
item.description: Jurors asked to review several videos and other evidence Wednesday as they continued deliberating the case against Daniel Penny in the death of Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department and the city of Memphis to address results of scathing police investigation
item.description: A federal investigation has found that the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols by officers after he ran away from a traffic stop in 2023 exposed serious problems in the Memphis Police Department, from the use of excessive force to the mistreatment of Black people in the majority-Black city.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz, Jonathan Mattise And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Associated Press’ top photos of 2024 from the US
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The right frame can freeze a moment in time, creating meaning for the masses from a fist pump over a bloodied ear, a bridge crumbled by a ship, towns shredded by nature, and a victory sealed with an on-field kiss. In 2024, photographers across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 2 kindergarteners wounded and gunman dead after shooting at California religious school
item.description: PALERMO, Calif. (AP) — Two children were in “extremely critical condition” Wednesday after being shot at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and the gunman died at the scene, apparently from a self-inflicted gunshot, police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bitcoin tops $100,000 as big rally sparked by Trump election win rolls on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin has topped the $100,000 mark as a massive rally in the world's most popular cryptocurrency sparked by the election of Donald Trump rolls on.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about martial law and the effort to impeach South Korea's president
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — South Korea's President Yoon Suk Yeol is facing possible impeachment for sending heavily armed soldiers into Seoul's streets with a baffling declaration of martial law that reminded many of the country’s past military-backed dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's boss in Manhattan
item.description: Investigators are searching for clues that could help them identify the masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies on a Manhattan sidewalk, then disappeared into Central Park.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Karen Matthews And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Delta stowaway apprehended by FBI at JFK airport upon landing in New York
item.description: A Russian national and legal resident of the United States who illegally stowed away on a Delta Airlines flight to Paris was taken into FBI custody at JFK International airport today.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California Christian school shooting wounds 2 boys, 5 and 6; suspected gunman dead
item.description: Two California private school students, ages 5 and 6, were shot Wednesday, and the suspected gunman is dead, according to authorities and reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Search intensifies for missing Pennsylvania grandmother as crews encounter unstable mine shafts
item.description: The search for Pennsylvania grandmother Elizabeth Pollard continued on Wednesday. Pollard, 64, is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole on Dec. 2 while searching for her pet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is it hotter than the sun outside? Wacky weather app bug says it is
item.description: If you took a look at the weather widget on your Windows computer Wednesday afternoon, you may have noticed that the numbers were higher than normal for winter.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, KRON4

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's state Supreme Court election next spring already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mother fights for answers after Army son's mysterious death
item.description: Anna Stanley's 23-year-old son Quandarius was seriously hurt while delivering aid on an American-built pier in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Annette Peagler, NewsNation

item.title: With dad on deployment, business decorates his family's home for holidays
item.description: Dawn Baslee, mother of three, told NewsNation affiliate WAVY this is the second Christmas her husband will miss with family because of a deployment.
item.dc:creator: Madison Pearman, NewsNation

item.title: Puppy food sold in 7 states recalled due to salmonella contamination: FDA
item.description: A litter of puppies became ill after eating the mix, a customer told the Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, Fox 5

item.title: Manhattan DA's downplaying of Daniel Penny's potential punishment 'improper and misleading': defense
item.description: What's behind Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's push to downplay the potential consequences for Daniel Penny, if his office gets a conviction in the Jordan Neely case?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christopher Dontell pleads guilty to charges in 2020 killing of Gregory Rice
item.description: Rice's body was found in the Pee Dee River on Nov. 8, 2020, according to authorities who said he had been shot multiple times.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Bright, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors: DC police officer’s talk with Proud Boys leader grew secretive as arrest neared
item.description: Metropolitan Police Department Lt. Shane Lamond’s communication with the former leader of the Proud Boys increased and grew more secretive as pressure to arrest him mounted in 2020, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Wendy's customers can get free Frostys for a year when they purchase this key tag
item.description: Wendy’s is giving away the gift of free Frosty treats this holiday season. On Wednesday, the chain announced that its Frosty Key Tags will be available for purchase in its restaurants and app from Dec. 9 until Feb. 15, 2025. For $3, customers can get unlimited free Jr.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: California farmers are hopeful Trump administration will deliver more water to fields
item.description: Since winning the election, President-elect Donald Trump has been talking about immigration, border security and government efficiency. But in California farm country, his comments about water are also getting top attention.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s pick to run the DEA withdraws his name from consideration. Trump says he pulled the plug
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, has withdrawn his name from consideration for the job, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being selected for a position requiring Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Surveillance video shows Cash App founder using knife to snort cocaine before death, defense alleges
item.description: Lawyers for Nima Momeni, who is accused of killing Cash App founder Bob Lee, played a new surveillance video during their closing arguments in his murder trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's onions is over: CDC
item.description: An outbreak across 14 states in October connected to McDonald’s slivered onions from the popular menu item impacted at least 100 people.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, Fox 5

item.title: Taco Bell adding photo booth experience to drive-thrus nationwide for a limited time
item.description: Select Taco Bell drive-thrus nationwide will be transformed into a “one of a kind” photo booth experience to capture fans’ everyday “Live Mas” moments. Taco Bell rewards members in California, Ohio, Tennessee, Texas and Florida will be able to participate in the drive-thru photo booth experience.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Florida woman who left boyfriend in zipped suitcase for hours sentenced in his death
item.description: She was “shocked” by the guilty verdict and thought maybe the jury would convict her of a lesser charge, her lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: First transgender attorney to argue before the Supreme Court, challenging health care ban for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the Supreme Court this week wades into the contentious issue of transgender rights, the justices will hear from an attorney with knowledge that runs deep.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: US Coast Guard is searching for 5 people after a fishing boat reportedly capsized in seas off Alaska
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard said Sunday it's searching for five people after a fishing boat reportedly capsized in rough weather and seas near Alaska's capital of Juneau.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, Fox 5
